database_constraint_checking.description: known relational schema with a parity CHECK constraint per record handle
database_constraint_checking.reason: The behavior is reproduced by ordinary online insertion into a known schema with a deferred-but-prefix-checkable parity constraint.

fixed_H_record_update.description: fixed finite state space with allowed subspace parity projector
fixed_H_record_update.reason: A fixed finite state space over the two handles can contain the parity-allowed record states from the start; update is projection/filtering, not H growth.

fixed_latent_source_changing_access.description: completed latent parity table with observers receiving partial apertures
fixed_latent_source_changing_access.reason: The full admissible table for h0 and h1 can be fixed from the start; observers only gain access to local projections as proposals arrive.

fixed_site_sheaf_gluing.description: fixed cover with local sections and parity compatibility on overlaps
fixed_site_sheaf_gluing.reason: The observer patches, handle set, bit projections, and parity compatibility predicate are all fixed in advance. Prefix extendability rejects p3 exactly.

ordinary_causal_order_only.description: partial order of proposal dependencies without a value compatibility predicate
ordinary_causal_order_only.reason: A causal partial order can say p3 occurs after p1 and p2, but order alone does not know that x_BC=0 violates parity. It needs an added compatibility predicate.

summary.strongest_survivor: clock-free record coherence is a useful reconstruction-layer discipline, but E096 C is fully reproduced by fixed compatibility/admissibility machinery

verdict.path_kill: E096 clock-free coherence by itself => source-side Temporal Issuance evidence
